short_term.user_input: Which limits are breached (if any), what immediate operational restrictions must be applied, and by what deadline must the bank complete full identification (and what happens if it doesn’t)? short_term.agent_response:
## 1) Applicable regulatory construct
The limits / restrictions and the one-year deadline you are asking about arise **specifically for “Accounts opened using Aadhaar OTP based e-KYC, in non-face-to-face mode”** and the conditions imposed on such accounts. [CDD Procedure in case of Individuals, p.29]

short_term.agent_response:
## 1) Limits that may be breached (and how to test breach)
The following limits apply to **“accounts opened using Aadhaar OTP based e-KYC, in non-face-to-face mode”** [A. CDD Procedure in case of Individuals (p.29), p.29]:

### Deposit-side limits
- **Aggregate balance cap (across all deposit accounts of the customer): ₹1,00,000**. If the customer’s **aggregate balance exceeds ₹1,00,000**, the bank must apply the specified operational restriction (see Section 2 below). [A. CDD Procedure in case

short_term.user_input: Show the latest Board-approved KYC policy and map each of the 4 mandatory elements (Customer Acceptance, Risk Management, CIP, Monitoring) to operating procedures, systems, and MI.
short_term.agent_response:
## 1) What the **latest (current) Board‑approved KYC Policy** must contain under RBI Directions 2025
